hypothesis: Deploying a swarm of LLM agents with systematically varied model temperatures to explore the same ALP reactor simulation in parallel, with a meta-agent that dynamically weights and synthesizes their findings based on experimental novelty scores, will achieve greater coverage of rare chemical interaction regimes (such as passivation-protected ALE cycles) within a fixed total experimental time budget than either a single high-capability agent or multiple agents running independently without cross-communication.
